meeting note 
1. List part order   
   1. Understand dimension   
2. Create schematic and pcb layout  
   1. Handoff design constraint to design team   

completed to this part (Mar 21)

3. Testing   
   1. Iterate if wrong  
4. Printing pcb  
5. Put together all components 

Plant Companion V0.01

Hardware **(To buy)** 

Programming interface (direct connect) 

**Power: Battery — 4 double A batteries (needs 6-9 volts of power)**

  **(LOWKEY THO: should be easier to power via usb for the prototype, once we move to v2, we can get esp32, power system, more sensor connections, and a custom design).** 

**MCU: Arduino Uno R3**

Sensors: 1\. Moisture sensor **DFRobot SEN0193** — slightly higher quality version of the same thing, \~$4  
[**​​https://mm.digikey.com/Volume0/opasdata/d220001/medias/docus/2107/SEN0193\_Web.pdf**](https://mm.digikey.com/Volume0/opasdata/d220001/medias/docus/2107/SEN0193_Web.pdf) **(Buy this one)**   

**Dimension list:** 

Sensor: 3.86 inches by 0.905 inches Thickness is negligible 

Arduino Uno: 68.6 mm in length and 53.4 mm in width. The board has a maximum height of roughly 12 mm, though total clearance required for components (like USB and DC barrel jack) is usually considered to be around 15 mm.

DC Battery   
![][image1]

	

Software : 

1st time user onboarding

1. Log in   
2. Take photo of plant   
   1. CV   
   2. Predict plant type  
3. Connect hardware to the webapp   
   1. (no clue how this would happen)   
      1. Figure out via research  
4. Get soil data   
   1. Predict ML (correlation between soil moisture and plant health)   
5. Return optimal watering schedule \+ health 

**For V2, customize pcb to fit data collection with more sensors instead of arduino.** 

Plant Companion V0.02
